expect.note: Informational: resolve repo deps for the foreign-cc wrapping target.
rules_foreign_cc cmake() expands to a macro; the underlying rule kind name may vary. Informational confirmation of structure.

Core success: the single coarse cmake() wrapping target builds Neovim's own CMake tree. High risk: code-gen needs host Lua, and bundled deps (libuv/luajit/lpeg/treesitter/unibilium/utf8proc) are fetched by the separate cmake.deps subproject and are NOT provided to this wrapped build, so this may require host-installed deps or fail (predicted_tier=1).
B-foreign has no fine-grained graph and no kind(..._test) targets. This asserts the foreign build's declared output (out_binaries=[nvim]) exists. Informational since it depends on tier-2 building.

No Bazel-wired test target exists. Neovim's own test suite is the busted/functional/unit suite driven by cmake/RunTests.cmake (needs the built nvim binary + Lua test runner + runtime), which is not exposed as a bazel test target in this coarse foreign wrap. No worker-authored smoke test should count here; honest expectations cap at tier-2 (build) / tier-3 (output exists). Tier-4 is informational only.
